rulings.Case Title: HERBERT SCHMELTZ VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 23, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Flowserve US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. As to identification of Durametallic packing, when read together, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration create a triable issue whether the packing at issue was Durametallic packing. As to asbestos content, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration, the only evidence defendant relied upon on this topic, are insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Smith's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY COLTON VS. CERTAINEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 23, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Flowserve US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 as to product identification. As to asbestos content of the Durametallic packing, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration, the only evidence defendant relied upon on this topic, are insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Smith's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND COLMAN ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Mary Colman As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
